features.name: Authentication and role access
features.description: Establish email/password authentication, role-aware entry points, and scoped access control for all protected product surfaces.
steps: Establish the authentication flow and session model for the supported role set.
Route authenticated users into role-appropriate entry experiences.
Enforce role-scoped access across protected views and data.

features.name: Event lifecycle management
features.description: Create and manage the event or programme record that anchors every downstream planning workflow.
steps: Model event records and lifecycle states.
Create authorized event creation and editing flows.
Use the event state as the root context for downstream planning data.

features.name: Department and sub-team structure
features.description: Model departments and sub-teams inside events so ownership and visibility follow the real church planning hierarchy.
steps: Model departments and sub-teams beneath each event.
Assign ownership for department heads and sub-leaders inside the hierarchy.
Restrict structure management to authorized planning scopes.

features.name: Volunteer onboarding and profile setup
features.description: Guide new volunteers through the minimum setup needed to participate in the rostering system.
steps: Gate new volunteer users into an onboarding path before the main dashboard.
Capture baseline profile details, interests, availability signals, and optional skill input.
Transition completed volunteers into the role-appropriate dashboard experience.

features.name: Availability and blockout management
features.description: Allow volunteers to maintain service availability and make that information usable during roster planning.
steps: Capture dated availability plus broader preference or blockout signals.
Make availability editable over time for volunteers.
Surface relevant availability inside leader planning views.

features.name: Interest request management
features.description: Allow volunteers to express interest in serving areas and route those requests to the correct leader for review.
steps: Let volunteers submit interest requests for departments or sub-teams.
Route each request to the appropriate reviewing leader.
Reflect request outcomes back to the volunteer and relevant planning views.

features.name: Skill profile and approval
features.description: Capture department-linked volunteer skills and require leader approval before those skills affect planning.
steps: Define department-linked skills and the volunteer skill submission flow.
Add leader approval and rejection handling for submitted skills.
Ensure only approved skills feed planning logic.

features.name: Roster planning and assignment management
features.description: Give leaders structured control over assigning volunteers into events, departments, and sub-teams.
steps: Create assignment records tied to event structure and volunteer context.
Expose relevant planning context while leaders assign or adjust rosters.
Treat removal and reassignment as controlled high-impact actions.

features.name: Skill-gap detection and planning signals
features.description: Surface leader-visible staffing or skill coverage risk before the event occurs.
steps: Define required skill coverage rules for planning scopes.
Compare coverage against approved skills and current roster state.
Surface actionable gap signals in leader planning views.

features.name: Personalized weekly dashboard
features.description: Provide each role with a weekly operational dashboard that shows only the context they need.
steps: Build role-specific weekly dashboard states.
Populate dashboards with scoped assignments, planning health, and relevant context.
Reflect assignment and planning state changes quickly enough for weekly coordination.

features.name: Instructions and media sharing
features.description: Let leaders publish event-specific guidance, notes, and attachments to the right audience.
steps: Create the instruction publishing flow tied to event, department, or sub-team context.
Support attachment of images and documents.
Restrict visibility to the intended recipients.

features.name: Assignment response workflow
features.description: Close the loop between assignment creation and volunteer acknowledgement with controlled status changes.
steps: Provide accept and decline actions from assignment-driven experiences.
Require confirmation on decline and enforce controlled assignment status progression.
Reflect response outcomes back into leader and volunteer weekly views.

features.name: Notifications and scheduled alerts
features.description: Notify volunteers and leaders when assignment state changes and send pre-event operational alerts.
steps: Trigger notifications for assignment creation and response changes.
Schedule and send the required 2-day and 5-day leader alerts.
Prevent stale or invalid notification delivery.

features.name: Admin oversight, soft delete, and approval controls
features.description: Route destructive actions through soft delete and admin review so operational safety is preserved.
steps: Route destructive changes through a soft-delete state.
Create the admin review path for permanent removal decisions.
Keep inactive records out of normal operations while preserving review visibility.

features.name: Error handling and support escalation
features.description: Make failures understandable and recoverable with calm messaging, clear next steps, and support escalation paths.
steps: Define calm, actionable error states across core user flows.
Add leader contact and bug-report escalation paths where required.
Prevent failure states from leaking protected data or breaking scope rules.
